steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage’s key signal is Tom’s statement that they “mean nothing but a little amusement among ourselves, just to vary the scene, and exercise our powers in something new.”

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A captures this clue: it states that Tom offers assurance that the play will be harmless and limited to a small group.
The other three either introduce ideas not mentioned—such as lack of skill, changed intentions, or promotion—or reverse the relationship.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
